title: Instrumentality making gods and returning from the LCL sea
display_title: Instrumentality making gods and returning from the LCL sea
wikitext:
[]: Instrumentality, making gods, and returning from the LCL sea             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Wed May 21, 2014 10:27 pm I've been wondering what the being would be like, specifically whether it would be mostly evil or mostly good, or would it be the type that believe itself to be beyond good and evil? What do you Good Mams and Sirs think? Another way to ask this question for me is, do you think this being would have more bad memories than good or more good memories than bad? Or do you believe that they would even out?

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed May 21, 2014 10:30 pm It would just sit there and do nothing. That's why it's bad and SEELE are poo poo.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Wed May 21, 2014 10:33 pm ^ Not what I asked Gud Ser Broham , but I accept your view and value it beyond reason

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Wed May 21, 2014 11:18 pm By it's very nature it is something entirely beyond our comprehension. It's a godhead. Like, it's literally beyond human conception. Any attempt at understanding it in such a way would be reducing it to such a level that it's incomparable.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Wed May 21, 2014 11:34 pm ^ Meh, I guess someone could go the whole "God is Unknowable" route but at least ponder it if you are able Good Sir.

[]: Re: The Singular Being Created From Instrumentality             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu May 22, 2014 12:03 am ChaddyManPrime wrote: I've been wondering what the being would be like, specifically whether it would be mostly evil or mostly good, or would it be the type that believe itself to be beyond good and evil?

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Thu May 22, 2014 12:08 am ^ Oh I see your point Good Sir, I got to make the question as to where people can't side-step it.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu May 22, 2014 12:29 am It's described as being a massive conglomerate, but I don't think it would have one supreme consciousness. Imagine a ton of tiny voices that are trying to control everything to the point where nothing useful gets done and everything starts bouncing off of everything else like a giant echo chamber.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Thu May 22, 2014 12:36 am ^ Oh, from what I gathered everything we are gets exchanged with one another to the point that we are no longer individuals but one massive unified mind.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu May 22, 2014 12:51 am A hivemind in the sense that everybody is connected to one another, yes. However, from what is portrayed both in the TV ending and in the movies, people do not control one another's actions or thoughts.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Thu May 22, 2014 12:55 am ^ I never said they do bro. Where are you getting that idea from? Point it to me so I can rewrite it please.

[]:             Quote Post by TomasJC » Thu May 22, 2014 12:58 am I think it would be that whole singular being kind of thing. Everyone's thoughts would create an incomprehensible (well to us anyway) single omnipresent being, where the collective feelings, thoughts and knowledge of earth and its inhabitants become of a single voice (i.e this godlike "brain" knows all that is possible to know from every being that combines). Wow, now all I can see is the evil Brain Spawn from Futurama...

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu May 22, 2014 12:59 am Except we know that humans are capable of perceiving themselves as a distinct individual mind, as observed in both EoE and EoTV.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Thu May 22, 2014 1:05 am ^ Oh okay I think I see where the dysfunction is now Good Sir. They don't have that small bit of independent thought that Shinji had Lilith give them in order for them to reject Instrumentality.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu May 22, 2014 2:20 am ^ Well, they could have that bit of independent thought, after all Asuka, Misato... talked to him during Instrumentality, but the difference is that even with that individuality they couldn't affect Instrumentality, the only one who could is Rei/Lilith, and she was only listening to Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Thu May 22, 2014 2:26 am ^ Broham, please tell me what you are talking about? I am very confused. I'm not asking how they affect Instrumentality.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu May 22, 2014 3:40 am ^ You said that "They don't have that small bit of independent thought that Shinji had Lilith give them in order for them to reject Instrumentality." But the events of EoE showed us that they did have that bit of independent thought, since they interacted with Shinji and even confronted him, if they didn't had any independent thoughts, they would had acted like drones talking about becoming one and other meta-physical BS. At least that was in the beginning, maybe had Shinji not rejected Instrumentality, their individuality would had faded little by little and merged with everyone else. But still, even with independent thoughts, the only one that could reject and destroy Instrumentality was Shinji, because the only being that could physically destroy Instrumentality, Rei/Lilith, was only listening and obeying to him.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Thu May 22, 2014 8:47 am ^ Broham I meant in this scenario. In this scenario they do not have the ability of independent thought just like it was originally planned. This is just concerning what the being would be like, please read OP if you do not understand.

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Thu May 22, 2014 8:54 am Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: A hivemind in the sense that everybody is connected to one another, yes. However, from what is portrayed both in the TV ending and in the movies, people do not control one another's actions or thoughts.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu May 22, 2014 9:35 am I can't say I've watched Star Trek, so I'm afraid don't know how accurate the comparison is.

What do you Good Mams and Sirs think? Another way to ask this question for me is, do you think this being would have more b
